finding: The backlog can lag behind source reality when the source fix lands first and the validator or documentation closeout lands later, but there was no quick repo-level scan to highlight open rows whose prose already sounds effectively fixed.
expectedBehavior: `doctor` should include a lightweight backlog/source drift scan so maintainers notice when bug-tracking status needs explicit closeout or follow-up evidence.
evidenceOrRepro: Repaired on 2026-06-28 by adding a `backlog-sync` doctor check that parses `docs/governance/atm-bug-and-optimization-backlog.md`, looks for open rows whose evidence/follow-up text already implies a landed fix, and emits a focused reminder to update the backlog status or land the missing validator/documentation closeout.
followUp: Keep the backlog-sync scan lightweight and heuristic, and tune phrases only when they clearly indicate source reality has outrun backlog state.
